channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Katsina Police Warn Applicants on Proper Conduct Ahead of Constable Exam
item.description: The Katsina State Police Command don advise all candidates wey dey prepare for the upcoming constable recruitment exam make dem follow the correct procedures sharply to avoid wahala with fraudsters. T...

item.title: Murang'a University Student Dies After Meeting Man from Dating Site
item.description: Fresh developments have emerged regarding the tragic death of 22-year-old Murang'a University student, Consolata Githinji, popularly known as 'Connie'. The young influencer fell from the sixth floor o...

item.title: Government calls for qualified professionals to register as irrigation service providers ahead of July 2026 regulations
item.description: The Ministry of Water, Sanitation, and Irrigation, through the State Department for Irrigation, has invited applications from qualified diploma and degree holders to be enlisted as professional irriga...

item.title: Kelowna community restores memorial garden ahead of National Day of Mourning
item.description: In Kelowna, a memorial garden at Knowles Heritage Park that honours five men killed in a 2021 crane collapse has been restored after being vandalized. The act of vandalism, which snapped off three of ...

item.title: Ghanaian man denies involvement in death of British widow scammed out of £1 million in romance fraud
item.description: Janet Fordham, a 69-year-old retired British housekeeper and widow from Devon, was tragically scammed out of her entire £1 million life savings by international romance fraudsters operating across the...

item.title: BP Reports Substantial Profit Growth Amid Global Oil Market Changes
item.description: BP, one of the world's largest oil companies, has reported a substantial rise in its profits in its latest financial results, marking the first report since the escalation of conflict in Iran. The com...

item.title: Punjab Assembly Passes New Law to Ban Child Marriages and Penalize Offenders
item.description: The Punjab Assembly has passed the Punjab Child Marriage Restraint Bill 2026, making marriage under the age of 18 a cognisable offence. The law stipulates that any adult above the age of 18 found marr...

item.title: Federal High Court Introduces E-Filing System in Lagos to Improve Court Efficiency
item.description: The Federal High Court has launched an e-filing system in the Lagos Division, transitioning from traditional paper-based processes to digital operations. This move aims to boost efficiency, transparen...

item.title: A Ground Report from Bhangar: Nawsad Siddique’s Political Influence Across Communities
item.description: In a detailed report from Bhangar, West Bengal, the article explores the political rise of MLA Nawsad Siddique, leader of the Indian Secular Front (ISF), who defied political odds in the 2021 state el...

item.title: Sweden Criticized by UN for Deporting Child with Severe Disabilities
item.description: The UN Human Rights Committee don yan Sweden serious talk for how dem deport one pikin wey get plenty-plenty health issues. The pikin, whose name na E. B., now 21 years old, been diagnosed with plenty...

item.title: Queensland Rail unions reject pay offer, warn of industrial action ahead of Magic Round
item.description: Queensland’s peak rail union has rejected the latest pay offer from Queensland Rail and announced plans to escalate their industrial campaign, including possible strike action during the Magic Round w...

item.title: Edmonton man faces trial over gun sales linked to police officers’ deaths and Pizza Hut shooting
item.description: In Edmonton, a trial has begun for Dennis Tyler Okeymow, 21, who faces 17 charges in connection with the deaths of two Edmonton Police Service officers and a Pizza Hut shooting in March 2023. The teen...

item.title: German Tourist Dies Suddenly at Mandela Memorial in KwaZulu-Natal
item.description: A 59-year-old German tourist tragically passed away after suffering a cardiac arrest at the Nelson Mandela memorial site in Howick, KwaZulu-Natal on Monday afternoon. Emergency services, including par...

item.title: Google to set up ₹1.35 lakh crore AI data centre in Visakhapatnam with Chandrababu Naidu laying foundation
item.description: On Tuesday, Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu inaugurated the foundation stone for Google's upcoming AI data centre at Tarluvada, on the outskirts of Visakhapatnam. This project is among the largest...

item.title: Google launches construction of major AI hub in Visakhapatnam, India
item.description: Tech giant Google has officially commenced work on its largest artificial intelligence hub outside the United States, located in Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh, India. The project, which was announced ...

item.title: Welsh Labour confronts potential loss and internal challenges ahead of May Senedd election
item.description: Welsh Labour, historically dominant in Wales, is confronting a potentially historic defeat in the forthcoming Senedd elections after 27 years in power. Polls indicate a significant decline in support,...

item.title: Seven killed in attack on Benue community as many others injured
item.description: At least seven people were killed in a fresh attack by armed invaders at Channel One, a settlement in Ukemberagya/Tswarev Council Ward of Logo Local Government Area in Benue State. The attack, which o...

item.title: Golden Knights Secure Dramatic Overtime Win Over Mammoth in Playoff Series
item.description: Shea Theodore scored a crucial overtime goal with 51.5 seconds left, giving the Vegas Golden Knights a 5-4 victory over the Utah Mammoth on Monday night. The win tied the first-round playoff series at...

item.title: Social media missteps and political oddities on Anzac Day
item.description: In a curious mix of politics and social media mishaps, Labor Senator Helen Polley found herself in hot water over a deleted Anzac Day tribute that controversially paired the Australian national anthem...

item.title: Mumbai: Security Guards Stabbed in Naya Nagar Following Religious Dispute
item.description: A deeply disturbing incident has unfolded in Naya Nagar, Mumbai, where two security guards, identified as Rajkumar Mishra and Subroto Sen, were allegedly stabbed by a man named Zaib Zuber Ansari. Acco...
